stemHtml: You’re flying Amsterdam (EHAM) to Alicante (LEAL). In the FMS you entered a forecast wind of 340°/60 kt, but the real wind is 260°/60 kt. The FMS is NOT updated with the new wind information. How this mismatch affects the FMS’s ETA and fuel predictions?

options.a: FMS calculates a later than actual landing time, fuel consumption calculation too high.
options.b: ETA of FMS is too early, fuel consumption calculation too low.
options.c: ETA of FMS is correct, fuel consumption calculation too low.
options.d: ETA of FMS is too late, fuel consumption calculation too high.
